title: Public Health (Water Borne, Water Washed and other Water Related Diseases Prevention) Regulations, 2018
title_en: Public Health (Water Borne, Water Washed and other Water Related Diseases Prevention) Regulations, 2018

provisions.title: Citation and commencement
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Public Health (Water Borne, Water Washed and Other Water Related Diseases Prevention) Regulations, 2018 and shall come into operation on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these regulations, unless the context requires otherwise— " Act " means the Public Health Act; " Authorized officer " has the meaning as ascribed to it under the Act ; " Authority " has the meaning as ascribed to it under the Act ; " clean and safe water " means any water which is rendered harmless and free from contaminants or treated water supplied for the consumption of human beings and meets water quality standards; " consumer " means a person to whom water is supplied by a water supplier or water vendor ; " Environmental Health Practitioner " has the meaning as ascribed under the Act ; " hygiene " refers to conditions and practices that help to maintain health and prevent the spread of diseases; " other water related diseases " refers to water based and water related insect vector diseases; " Person " has a meaning as ascribed to it under the Interpretation of laws Act ; [Cap. 1] " Premises " has the meaning as ascribed to it under the Act ; " recreational water " include pools, hot tubs and bathing beaches, at lakes, rivers and ocean; " sanitary accommodation " has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; " water contaminants " means any physical, chemical, biological or radiological substance or matter in water that may adversely affect human health upon exposure; " water disinfectant " means a chemical that destroys harmful micro-organisms and parasites in water as approved by the Government Chemist; " Water disinfection " means the process of water treatment to:— (a) remove; (b) kill; and render harmless to human health every pathogenic micro-organisms and parasite that would otherwise be present in the water; " water reserve tank " means a water supply facility used for storing water for domestic purposes; " water safety plan " means comprehensive risk assessment and risk management approach that includes all steps in the water supply from catchment to consumer; " water supplier " means a person authorized to supply water; " water supply " means the provision of appropriate facilities and services for sourcing, treatment and distribution of portable water; " water treatment " means any process that makes water more acceptable for a specific end-use; " water vendor " means a person who sells water from wells, water tanks, tankers, water receptacles and or any other water sources; " water-borne diseases " means diseases caused by ingestion of water contaminated by fecal or urine or any other harmful substances; " water-related diseases " include water borne, water washed, chemical, contaminants and other water related diseases ; " water-washed diseases " means diseases caused by inadequate use of water for hygiene and sanitation purposes. Part II – Administration and operationalization

provisions.title: Duties and functions of the authority
provisions.content: (1) The Authority shall have the following duties and functions:— (a) ensure supply of clean and safe water to the public for drinking and domestic use; (b) maintain storm water drainage and sewerage systems for prevention of pollution and contamination of water sources and water supply systems; (c) plan and provide sites for establishing sanitary accommodation in public places; (d) ensure safe disposal of waste; (e) monitor water quality standards as prescribed in the guidelines; (f) monitor health promotion and education on safe water and water related disease prevention; (g) monitor water treatment and safe storage practices in premises as prescribed in the guidelines; (h) provide vaccination or prophylaxis to travelers and contacts for prevention of international notifiable diseases; (i) carry out surveillance, investigation and control of water related diseases; (j) ensure water suppliers and vendors have valid permits, license and registration certificate; (k) inspect wells, water tanks, tankers, water receptacles and any other water sources used for water supply or vending; (l) make follow up in premises to ensure compliance of water quality standards; (m) Ensure mandatory labelling of water tanks, tankers and any other water receptacles for water vending. (2) Authority shall take necessary measures as it may deem fit for prevention and control of water related diseases.

provisions.title: Duties Environmental Health Practitioners
provisions.content: The Environmental Health Practitioner shall have the following duties— (a) provide health education and promotion on safe water and water related diseases prevention; (b) inspect household water treatment and safe storage facilities for drinking water; (c) collect water samples at the water source, point of consumption or any other point for analysis as prescribed in the Third Schedule to these Regulations; (d) identify environmental health hazards and assess the risks in the households and in the community; (e) follow up contacts for water related diseases; (f) keep records and document information on water related diseases; (g) issue and withdraw notice on any operation regarding water safety; (h) supervise proper disposal of the infectious dead body; (i) inspect recreational water , wells, water supply systems and wastewater systems; (j) conduct sanitary surveys in water supply systems; (k) Conduct an investigation of any diseases which appears to be water related and make recommendations on prevention and control measures. (2) An Authorized officer shall notify the responsible authority on suspected water related diseases. [Please note: Numbering as in original.]

provisions.title: Duties of water suppliers
provisions.content: (1) The water supplier shall take necessary measures to ensure that:— (a) the prevention of pollution and contamination of water used by the public for domestic purposes derived from sources within or beyond the jurisdiction of the Authority ; (b) water supplied is safe and free from contaminants or pollutants; (c) water reserve tanks are cleaned and disinfected at least twice a year or as may be recommended by the Authority . (2) Water supplier shall carry out water quality risk assessment annually or as may be recommended by the Authority to determine the quality of water as prescribed in the guidelines.

provisions.title: Duties of water vendors
provisions.content: The water vendor shall take necessary measures to ensure that:— (a) abstraction of water from the approved sources; (b) water supplied to the consumer is not contaminated or polluted; (c) water tanks, tankers and other water receptacles used for storage and transportation of safe water are clean and free from contaminants; (d) water tanks, tankers and other water receptacles are labelled as per the responsible authority prescription; (e) water tanks, tankers and other water receptacles are of acceptable standards to prevent leakage, infiltration and contamination; (f) water reserve tanks are clean, disinfected and properly maintained.

provisions.title: Duties of consumer
provisions.content: The consumer shall take necessary measures to ensure— (a) availability of safe and clean water for drinking and domestic purposes; (b) cleanliness of drinking water storage containers; (c) proper personal hygiene is practiced; (d) general house environment is clean and maintained; (e) household water treatment and safe storage are practiced; (f) proper use of improved toilets; (g) functional hand washing facilities with running water and soap are available for use; (h) adherence to any directives provided by the Authority in relations to prevention and control of water related diseases.

provisions.title: Powers of authorized officer
provisions.content: An Authorized officer shall, in the course of conducting investigation on water related diseases do the following:— (a) order for cessation of use of any suspected contaminated water source pending the results of the laboratory examination; (b) require the removal or containment of the source of contamination; (c) require improvement of water treatment methods or procedures; (d) require water to be treated for human consumption; (e) demand for the cessation of distribution of water from contaminated water source; (f) require use of safe water from alternative source; (g) require refraining from doing of any other thing that may in his opinion lead to the spread of the infection; (h) order for the closure, or destruction of any water source that has been proven to be contaminated; (i) take water sample from water supplier and water vendors for monitoring water quality standards; (j) order the exclusion of any person suffering from water related diseases involved in the water supply and vending business. Part III – Prevention of water borne, water washed and other water related diseases

provisions.title: Water source and water supply infrastructure
provisions.content: A source of infrastructures for water supply used for human consumption shall be located, designed, constructed operated and maintained in such a way that does not cause water related diseases.

provisions.title: Mining supply of safe and clean water in premises
provisions.content: (1) All premises snail ensure availability of safe and clean water at least 25 liters per capital per day. (2) All premises or persons or organizers of public gatherings shall ensure water for drinking and other uses is safe and clean.

provisions.title: Categorization of water related diseases
provisions.content: (1) Water related diseases shall be categorized as follows— (a) Waterborne diseases; (b) water-washed diseases ; (c) water related diseases; and (d) diseases related to chemical contaminants. (2) Water related diseases are listed as ascribed in the First Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Prevention of water related diseases
provisions.content: Every person and head of households shall have a duty to prevent water related diseases by:— (a) ensuring water available in the households is safe and clean; (b) ensuring at least 25 liters of water per capita per day is available for domestic purposes; (c) ensuring animal quarters are separated from human living spaces as ascribed in the guidelines; (d) maintaining and servicing storm water drainage and sewerage systems in such a way that they do not allow mosquitoes and other vectors to breed; (e) maintaining properly wastewater treatment plant; (f) ensuring proper maintenance of water supply systems to control spillage, leakage and seepage; (g) planning, designing and construction of water management infrastructures; (h) preventing breeding of vectors through grading, filling and levelling of land surfaces; (i) ensuring proper management of water level fluctuations, water velocity changes, flushing and weed clearing in wastewater systems and water bodies; (j) keeping premises environment clean; and (k) ensuring proper management of stored water.

provisions.title: Prevention of chemical contaminants in water
provisions.content: A person shall, in prevention of chemical contaminants in water have a duty to— (a) ensure water supplied for domestic, agricultural, or animal keeping are free from harmful toxin substances or chemicals to human health; (b) allocate water supply sources away from the source of contaminants; (c) conduct risk assessment on contaminants; (d) communicate and submit water quality information on contaminants to the Authority ; (e) monitor water quality standards for contaminants; (f) conduct sanitary inspection.

provisions.title: Medical examination for water vendors
provisions.content: (1) A water vendor shall in prevention of water related diseases be medically examined in recognized health facility twice a year and be issued with a dully signed medical certificate. (2) Subject to sub regulation (1) any person who suffers from any water related diseases shall cease working until he has recovered.

provisions.title: Risk assessment plan
provisions.content: Any institution and water supplier owning an underground, ground or elevated water reserve tank shall be required by the Authority to possess a Risk assessment plan.

provisions.title: Water safety plan
provisions.content: Any authority responsible for water supply shall possess a water safety plan .

provisions.title: Swimming pool and other recreational water
provisions.content: Every person owning a swimming pool and other recreational water shall ensure that:— (a) water is safe and clean; (b) appropriate and approved disinfectants are used; (c) water treatment records and processes are kept and displayed. Part IV – General provision

provisions.title: Training
provisions.content: (1) Any person involved in the business of water, and recreational water and food preparation, processing and selling shall be trained on prevention of water related diseases, health and safety by qualified and registered person and institution. (2) Persons and institutions offering training shall be registered and accredited by Envirorimental Health Practitioners Registration Council. (3) Costs for training will be covered by the individual, owner or operator of such business.

provisions.title: Penalty
provisions.content: A person who contravenes any provision of these Regulations commits an offence and upon conviction shall be liable to a fine not exceeding five hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or both. First Schedule (Made under regulation 11) Water related diseases Water borne diseases include; Water washed diseases include; Other water related diseases include Diseases related to chemical contaminants in water include; cholera; soil-transmitted helminthes; schistomiasis; Arsenicosis amoeabiasis; diarrhea; dracunculiasis; Fluorosis giardiasis; dysentery; filariasis; Organs damage (liver, kidney, reproductive, endocrine) typhoid; cholera; threadworm; Thyroid disorders rotavirus infection; trachoma; malaria: Cancer campylobacteriosis scabies; yellow fever; Arsenicosis poliomyelitis skin infection; dengue fever; Fluorosis cryptosporidiosis; eye infection; onchocerciasis; and Organs damage (liver, kidney, reproductive, endocrine) hepatitis A and E; acute respiratory infections (ARI); trypanosomiasis Thyroid disorders E. coli infection Salmonellosis Dysentery Diarrhea viralgastroenteritis; Second Schedule (Made under regulation 4 and 8) Sampling water protocol for microbiological analysis A. Sampling from a tap or pump outlet 1. Clean the tap i) Remove from the tap any attachments that may cause splashing ii) Using a clean cloth, wipe the outlet to remove any dirt. 2. Open the tap Turn on the tap at maximum flow and let the water run for 1-2 minutes Note : if the water sample is taken after stage 2 the results obtained will provide information on the quality of the water as consumed. However, if the procedure iscontinued to stages 3 and 4 the results represent the quality of the water excluding contamination by the tap (as the tap will be sterilized). 3. Sterilize the tap Sterilize the tap for a minute with the flame from a gas burner, cigarette lighter, or anignited alcohol-soaked cotton-wool swab. 4. Open the tap before sampling Carefully turn on the tap and allow the water to flow for 1 -2 minutes at a medium flowrate. Do not adjust the flow after it has been set 5. Open the sterilized bottle Take out a bottle and carefully unscrew the cap or pull out the stopper 6. Fill the bottle i) While holding the cap and protective cover face downwards ii) Immediately hold the bottle under the waterjet, and fill. iii) Leave a small air space to make shaking before analysis easier. 7. Stopper or cap the bottle Place the stopper in the bottle or screw on the cap and fix the brown paper protective cover in place with the string. B. Sampling from a storage reservoir i) Open the sterilized bottle ii) Holding the bottle by the lower part, submerge it to a depth of about 20cm, with the mouth facing slightly upwards iii) Then cap the bottle Third Schedule (Made under regulation 4) Water sampling and analysis form [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] History of this document 22 February 2019 amendment not yet applied Amended by Public Health (Water Borne, Water Washed And Other Water Related Diseases Prevention) (Amendments) Regulations, 2019 04 May 2018 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania Commenced 13 February 2018 Assented to Related documents Is amended by Public Health (Water Borne, Water Washed And Other Water Related Diseases Prevention) (Amendments) Regulations, 2019 More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
